methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $3.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $8.6B - $15.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

data.narrative:
Ayar Labs, a Series E-stage AI & Software company founded by Mark Wade, is valued at $3.75B post-money on $500M raised to date. The peer set consists of nine comparable companies across the AI & Software sector—one public (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private firms including Crusoe Energy, Waymo, Figure AI, and Applied Intuition.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $3.8B last post-money valuation and applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E-to-later advancement, implies $5.2B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Applied Intuition at $15.0B (Series F), Crusoe Energy at $10.0B (Series E), and Wayve at $8.6B (Series D)—yields a median implied value of $10.0B, with a range of $8.6B to $15.0B.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $4.5B (low) to $11.5B (high), with a median of $7.6B. The current post-money sits near the low end of this range. Key uncertainties include the dispersion within the peer set—particularly the $6.4B gap between Wayve and Applied Intuition—and the limited applicability of the single public comparable. Stage-progression methodology carries model risk given typical multiplier variability across market cycles and sector momentum.
